visual_checks: Check if text can be resized up to 200% without assistive technology using browser zoom
After zooming to 200%, verify no text is clipped, truncated, or overlapped
Look for text containers with fixed heights that cause text to overflow when enlarged
Check if horizontal scrolling is required to read text at 200% zoom (two-dimensional scrolling is a concern but see also 1.4.10)
Verify that all functionality remains available when text is zoomed to 200%
Look for images of text that cannot be resized by the browser
pass_conditions: Text can be resized up to 200% using standard browser zoom or text-size features without loss of content or functionality
Resized text is not clipped, truncated, or obscured by other content
All text content, including text in form controls, scales appropriately
Functionality is not lost when text is resized (buttons still work, forms still submit, navigation still functions)
Content containers expand to accommodate resized text
fail_conditions: Text is clipped or hidden when zoomed to 200% due to fixed-height containers with overflow:hidden
Text overlaps other text or UI elements at 200% zoom making it unreadable
Critical functionality is lost at 200% zoom (buttons hidden, navigation broken)
Text is implemented as images that do not scale with browser zoom
CSS uses absolute units (px) for container heights, preventing text containers from expanding
na_conditions: Content is exclusively video or audio media with no text interface
ne_conditions: AI response could not be parsed
Cannot test zoom behavior from a single static screenshot
Browser zoom functionality could not be tested in the current environment
common_mistakes: Fixed-height navigation bars that clip text at larger zoom levels
Tooltip text in fixed-size containers that overflow when zoomed
Maximum font-size set in CSS preventing text from scaling
Viewport meta tag with maximum-scale=1 preventing zoom on mobile devices
Card layouts with fixed heights that truncate content when text is enlarged
Using images of text for headings or buttons instead of styled HTML text

examples.pass: All body text, headings, and form labels resize proportionally to 200% using browser zoom, containers expand to accommodate the larger text, and all navigation and functionality remains intact
examples.fail: A navigation menu has a fixed height of 50px — when text is zoomed to 200%, the menu text is cut off and users cannot see or click on menu items below the visible area
examples.partial: Most content resizes properly at 200% zoom, but a sidebar widget with fixed dimensions clips its text content and a dropdown menu becomes unusable

plain_meaning: Users must be able to enlarge text up to 200% using browser zoom or text-resize features without losing content or breaking functionality. Captions and images of text are exempt. The intent is that users with mild-to-moderate low vision can read pages without needing screen magnification software.
scope_applies_to: Body text, headings, labels, and all rendered textual content in the main document
Form labels, button text, and link text
Text inside modals, popovers, and expandable regions
Text inside iframes when part of the page experience
scope_does_not_apply_to: Visually-hidden / sr-only text clipped to 1px (no rendered box can overflow or clip at 200% zoom)
Elements removed from the accessibility tree (aria-hidden, display:none, visibility:hidden, hidden attribute)
Images of text (explicitly exempt; covered by SC 1.4.5)
Reflow at narrow viewports (that belongs to SC 1.4.10)
Text spacing adjustments via user stylesheet (that belongs to SC 1.4.12)
Content inside canvas or third-party embeds whose text rendering is out of the author's control
borderline_calibration: Content that remains readable at 200% browser zoom without loss of information or functionality PASSES, even if the layout reflows or wraps.
Text clipping that only affects visually-hidden sr-only content is NOT a failure. That text has no rendered box.
Horizontal scrolling at 200% on a desktop viewport is not automatically a failure unless information or functionality is lost.
If the page provides a native text-resize widget (not zoom) that achieves 200% without loss, that also satisfies the SC.
Do NOT flag fixed pixel heights on visually-hidden boxes (1px) as resize failures.
